scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All pass criteria met: throughput gap calculated precisely (300/min vs 15-20/min), unbounded queue growth described with specific numbers (16,000+/hr), visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with math (10×4s=40s>30s), missing DLQ identified as a hard blocker, queue depth metric gap called out as CRITICAL, autoscaling to 15-20 instances recommended, verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Throughput math is precise and well-articulated — 280 messages/min growth rate, 16,800/hr backlog projection
Visibility timeout analysis goes beyond identification — calculates worst-case batch time (40s) vs timeout (30s) and explains the duplicate delivery mechanism
DLQ absence framed correctly as a hard blocker with concrete impact: poison messages consuming capacity in tight retry loops
Queue depth metric gap elevated to CRITICAL with operational reasoning — on-call engineer flying blind
Exceptionally thorough resilience assessment covering six distinct failure scenarios with concrete recommendations
Goes beyond rubric requirements: identifies missing idempotency, circuit breakers, graceful degradation, rollback concerns, and memory exhaustion risk
Confidence section is well-calibrated — high confidence on deterministic math, low confidence on ambiguous areas like async I/O concurrency
